title: Screen time blocker apps: the new goldmine

slides.headline: Screen time blockers are printing money
slides.visual_note: Phone with lock icon, money bills floating around

slides.headline: The market size
slides.body: Digital wellness apps: $1.2B in 2025. Growing 15% annually. Parents, students, and remote workers all buying.

slides.headline: Why people pay for this
slides.body: Average person spends 7 hours/day on screens. They know it's a problem. They'll pay $50-100/year to fix it.
slides.visual_note: Clock showing 7 hours, stressed person icon

slides.headline: The top earners
slides.body: Forest: $400k/month. Opal: $200k/month. One Sec: $150k/month. All solo or small teams.

slides.headline: The winning formula
slides.body: Pick a niche (students, parents, faith). Add gamification (streaks, rewards). Make it pretty (cute mascots win). Charge $29-69/year.

slides.headline: Untapped niches
slides.body: Muslim focus timer (pray instead of scroll). Christian devotional blocker. Fitness unlock (walk to use phone). Study quiz gatekeeper.

slides.headline: Tech stack to build one
slides.body: React Native + Expo. Screen Time API (iOS) / UsageStats (Android). RevenueCat for subs. 2-4 weeks to MVP.
slides.visual_note: Tech logos in a stack diagram

slides.headline: Marketing that works
slides.body: TikTok before/after videos. Student study accounts. Parenting Facebook groups. Reddit r/nosurf community.
slides.visual_note: Social platform icons with arrows pointing to app

slides.headline: Why now
slides.body: Apple and Google added native screen time. But they suck. People want better UX and actual accountability.
slides.visual_note: Native iOS/Android icons with X, custom app with checkmark

cta_slide.headline: Building a focus app this month
